jalapeno-cilantro-ranch-dip.name: Jalapeño-cilantro Ranch Dip
instructions: Combine sour cream, cilantro, mayonnaise, jalapeños, and ranch dressing mix in a blender; blend until cilantro and peppers are finely minced and incorporated. You might need to stir it a few times with a spatula to make sure everything's well mixed. Serve immediately or refrigerate.

jalapeno-cilantro-yogurt-sauce.name: Jalapeño-cilantro Yogurt Sauce
instructions: Purée jalapeño, yogurt, cilantro, mint, lime juice, cumin, and a generous pinch of salt in a blender until very smooth. Taste and season with salt and more lime juice, if desired.

jericallas-traditional-mexican-recipe-co.name: Jericallas: Traditional Mexican Recipe
instructions: To start with, we'll get the oven up to 180 degrees Celsius.
Then we will warm up an anti-stick pot with the aim of boiling milk. The cinnamon and vanilla extract will also be heated in this liquid.
To continue, we will add the cup of sugar, we will revamp everything and we will let the mixture break into a boil.
Once milk has begun to boil, we will get it out of fire and let it rest at time (or at room temperature) so that it gets cold.

While this happens, we will pour the contents of the eggs into a bowl and beat them. Then we will add a few tablespoons of tibia milk to this mixture. We'll do it and get them mixed up, and we'll get them put up one by one. The idea is to blend very well, with no risk that the eggs will be cooked as a result of the remaining heat. That's about 7 or 8 tablespoons of milk.
Once we have obtained a uniform mixture, we will add them to the rest of the pot with milk. We will do this by pounding vigorously until all the ingredients are fully integrated.
The following will be to distribute production into individual molds. At this point we will remove cinnamon branches or cuts.
To put these mold into the oven, first we will prepare a great bowl with water and then we will put our small syringes to swim in a bathroom of Mary.

The complete container will then go to the pressurized oven. The cooking time may vary depending on the power of each oven. At first, we talk about about 45 minutes, but most important is to get our goal: to have the syringes curved. If they have been after this time, they still have to cook and have to leave them a little more.
optionally, if you want to put up the surface of the syringes, put up the oven grid to the highest level and let them cook there about 10 more minutes, increasing the temperature to 200 degrees.

juicy-baked-chicken-breast.name: Juicy Baked Chicken Breast

ingredients.item: Salt and freshly ground pepper to taste

instructions: Preheat the oven to 232°C (230 degrees C). Line a rimmed baking sheet with foil.

Drizzle olive oil over chicken breasts coating all sides. Combine sazonador total, garlic, salt, and pepper in a small bowl and sprinkle on all sides of the chicken breasts. Place chicken breasts onto the baking sheet.

Bake, uncovered, in the preheated oven until chicken breast is no longer pink in the center and the juices run clear, about 25 minutes. An instant-read thermometer inserted into the center should read at least 74°C (74 degrees C). Do not overbake. Allow to rest for 10 minutes before serving.

jumbo-chocolate-cupcakes.name: Jumbo Chocolate Cupcakes
instructions: Preheat the oven to 191°C (190 degrees C). Generously grease a 6-cup jumbo muffin pan with cooking spray.
Combine flour, cocoa powder, baking soda, baking powder, and salt in a bowl.
Beat butter until smooth in a large bowl. Add sugar and beat for 1 minute. Add eggs and vanilla and beat until thoroughly mixed. Stir in dry ingredients. Add milk and mix until smooth.
Fill the prepared muffin cups 3/4 full with batter.

Bake in the preheated oven until a toothpick inserted in the centers comes out clean, about 35 minutes. Cool in the pan for 5 minutes. Transfer to a wire rack and let cool completely, about 25 minutes more.
